frameworkVersion: Broker Operating Software Report Card v1.0

categories.name: Core broker value and positioning
categories.rationale: The product now presents one coherent map-first broker operating system rather than a collection of unrelated tools.
evidence: The landing page, authenticated shell, and primary navigation consistently center Today, Map, Pursuits, Activity, and Scorecard.
The retired launcher redirects into Today instead of forcing a tool-selection step.
Market Memory is framed as database health and spatial recall, not a second command center.

categories.name: Daily execution and workflow speed
categories.rationale: Today provides ranked work, waiting threads, review exceptions, development work, pipeline coverage, and a single best move in one scan.
evidence: Deterministic broker simulations cover morning triage, waiting work, review, and mobile use.
The public demo now loads a realistic ranked day instead of an empty desk.
The detailed follow-up route remains available as a drill-down but no longer competes in persistent navigation.

categories.name: Hands-off capture and integration
categories.rationale: Codex-assisted sends have a direct recorder with durable retry and Postmark remains a fallback, but continuous multi-source capture is not yet complete.
evidence: The Codex recorder supports email, call, meeting, and note activity with a durable local outbox.
The interface now describes Codex as the primary bridge and Postmark as fallback capture.
Calendar events, phone calls, tours, proposals, and full Outlook history are not yet all captured automatically in production.

categories.name: Data trust and explainability
categories.rationale: The ingestion architecture has strong evidence, confidence, idempotency, review, and terminal-stage safeguards.
evidence: Attempted or drafted email is not recorded as sent; delivery confirmation is required.
Uncertain matches stay in review and cannot create map pins or confirmed facts.
Provider identity and stable activity identity are preserved for retry safety.

categories.name: Map and market intelligence
categories.rationale: The deployed map supports Edmonton-focused discovery, unsaved address search, prospect context, and satellite mode, and the demo now includes useful market pins.
evidence: A deployed real-map run confirmed address search, result selection, map focus, and satellite mode.
Prospect editing and recent activity are available from the map panel.
The modern Places implementation is retained behind an explicit flag until the deployed browser key is allowed to use the new service, avoiding a doomed production request.

categories.name: Prospecting and pursuit system
categories.rationale: Pursuits are now clearly anchored to a listing, property, territory, owner target, or requirement, but the complete listing-activity-report loop is not finished.
evidence: Pursuit creation requires both a useful name and a property or market anchor.
Owned and shared pursuits show owner, role, prospect counts, and direct entry into the shared workspace.
Client-ready monthly activity report export remains a future workflow.

categories.name: Team collaboration
categories.rationale: Roles, sharing, ownership, and shared pursuit context exist, but the evidence is still simulated rather than a full live two-account proof.
evidence: Team lead, assistant, and junior-broker scenarios pass in the deterministic suite.
Viewer/editor/owner states and share controls are represented.
A real invitation, acceptance, concurrent edit, and handoff run has not yet been completed.

categories.name: Performance and reliability
categories.rationale: The core deterministic suite passes, production map behavior works, and the public demo no longer leaks authenticated API 401s across supporting screens.
evidence: Web production build completes successfully.
Desktop, mobile, and team broker simulations complete without critical accessibility or console failures.
Demo API responses are local, deterministic, and read safely without enabling a shared production demo account.

categories.name: UX coherence and accessibility
categories.rationale: The product now uses a consistent work rail, shared page headers, tighter language, and fewer competing destinations.
evidence: Persistent navigation is limited to five core jobs plus Requirements and Market Memory.
Activity and capture-health language matches the Codex-first, source-aware architecture.
Focused axe runs report no critical or serious violations in the simulated core journeys.

categories.name: Motivation and production feedback
categories.rationale: The Scorecard connects follow-up, prospecting, market memory, streaks, and pipeline coverage, but production conversion math is still shallow.
evidence: Today shows effort, momentum, pipeline coverage, follow-up completion, and a 28-day activity pulse.
Scorecard rewards controllable actions rather than only closed revenue.
Meeting-to-tour-to-proposal-to-offer conversion and production forecasting are not yet fully modeled.

gates.name: Core broker flow
gates.evidence: Today, Map, Pursuits, Activity, and Scorecard complete their primary simulated jobs.

gates.name: Factual integrity and capture honesty
gates.evidence: Review, provider identity, idempotency, delivery confirmation, and terminal-stage protections are enforced in code and API tests.

gates.name: Public demo reliability
gates.evidence: The public demo uses realistic local evidence and avoids authenticated API calls on audited screens.

gates.name: Production map
gates.evidence: Deployed search, result selection, map focus, and satellite mode pass without console errors; modern Places remains an opt-in migration.

gates.name: Hands-off capture claim
gates.evidence: Codex-assisted sends can record directly, but the advertised full activity universe is not yet automatically captured.

gates.name: Team-ready claim
gates.evidence: Deterministic team simulations pass; a real two-account collaboration run remains outstanding.

nextTuneUps.title: Make source capture observable
nextTuneUps.reason: Add last-success, last-failure, queued count, and source-specific freshness so hands-off operation is measurable rather than assumed.

nextTuneUps.title: Close the activity-to-pipeline loop
nextTuneUps.reason: Automatically classify meetings, tours, proposals, and offers so the dashboard can calculate the path to production.

nextTuneUps.title: Complete one live team proof
nextTuneUps.reason: Invite a second real account, test role boundaries and concurrent pursuit work, and export a client activity report.

nextTuneUps.title: Finish the modern Places migration
nextTuneUps.reason: Enable Places API (New) for the deployed browser key, turn on the feature flag, and rerun the real-map benchmark before retiring compatibility mode.

nextTuneUps.title: Add broker outcome telemetry
nextTuneUps.reason: Measure time to first action, unlogged activity, queue completion, follow-up aging, and conversion by stage.

evidenceLimits: Most workflow evidence comes from deterministic broker simulations and a single production environment, not a multi-broker field study.
No live two-account collaboration run was completed in this audit.
No phone-log integration was included, by design.
The hands-off capture score reflects architecture and focused tests; it is not a 30-day production reconciliation against Outlook and calendar activity.
